sample_narratives.issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
sample_narratives.text: There is an account on my credit report that is not mine. I believe this is the result of identity theft and I have disputed it twice with no correction.

sample_narratives.product: Credit reporting, credit repair services, or other personal consumer reports
sample_narratives.text: A fraudulent account was opened in my name. Despite a police report and a fraud alert, the item has not been removed from my report.

sample_narratives.product: Credit reporting, credit repair services, or other personal consumer reports
sample_narratives.text: My report shows a late payment that I never had. This error is hurting my score and I was denied a loan because of it.

components.label: Untimely responses
components.evidence: 1.5% of cases not answered on time

components.label: Closed without relief
components.evidence: 88.4% of closed cases ended without monetary or non-monetary relief

components.label: Issue concentration
components.evidence: 44.3% of complaints share one top issue

summary: Between 2025-01-01 and 2026-06-29, the CFPB published 1,600 complaints about Experian. Volume is steady (last 3 months averaged 110 per month versus 99 before). The most common issue is "Incorrect information on your report" (44% of complaints), concentrated in credit reporting, credit repair services, or other personal consumer reports. The company responded on time to 99% of cases; 88.4% of closed cases ended without relief. Overall risk signal: Elevated (58/100). This is an exploratory signal from public complaint data, not a regulatory, legal, or investment conclusion.
